latest.title: Faron Pharmaceuticals: Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd: Registration of Treasury Shares
latest.content:
Today 15:00
Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd | Company announcement | June 11, 2026 at 17:00:00 EEST
Capitalised terms used in this announcement have the meanings given to them in the announcement made on 28 May 2026 at 15:35 EEST regarding the issue of treasury shares to the Company, unless the context provides otherwise.
Turku, Finland
– Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing novel immunotherapies, as announced on 28 May 2026, issued 21,469,427 treasury shares to the Company itself without consideration to further prepare for any future conversions of the First and Second Tranche of Bonds.
In total, 21,469,427 new Treasury Shares in the Company have on 8 June 2026 been registered in the Finnish Trade Register. The treasury shares will rank pari passu in all respects with the existing shares of the Company. Following the registration of new Shares, the aggregate number of ordinary shares in the Company is 227,881,315 and of these shares, the Company holds 25,000,000 shares in treasury. The shares held in treasury by the Company do not confer a right to dividends or other shareholder rights.
The treasury shares will be listed on First North on 12 June 2026 and on AIM on or around 17 June 2026.
For more information, please contact:
IR Partners, Finland
(Media)
Kare Laukkanen
+358 50 553 9535 / +44 7 469 766 223
kare.laukkanen@irpartners.fi
FINN Partners, US
(Media)
Alyssa Paldo
+1 847 791-8085
alyssa.paldo@finnpartners.com
Cairn Financial Advisers LLP
(Nominated Adviser and Broker)
Sandy Jamieson, Jo Turner
+44 (0) 207 213 0880
Sisu Partners Oy
(Certified Adviser on Nasdaq First North)
Juha Karttunen
Jukka Järvelä
+358 (0)40 555 4727
+358 (0)50 553 8990
About Faron Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Faron (AIM: FARN, First North: FARON) is a global, clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focused on tackling cancers via novel immunotherapies. Its mission is to bring the promise of immunotherapy to a broader population by uncovering novel ways to control and harness the power of the immune system. The Company’s lead asset is bexmarilimab, a novel anti-Clever-1 humanized antibody, with the potential to remove immunosuppression of cancers through reprogramming myeloid cell function. Bexmarilimab is being investigated in Phase I/II clinical trials as a potential therapy for patients with hematological cancers in combination with other standard treatments. Further information is available at
www.faron.com.

company_30s.what_they_do: Faron Pharmaceuticals develops and markets targeted cancer therapies, mainly a monoclonal antibody called bexmab that re‑programs bone marrow to enhance the body’s own immune response against tumours.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The oncology market is growing faster than most other pharma sectors, and bexmab offers a novel mechanism that could complement existing checkpoint inhibitors. If the drug shows clinical benefit, it could capture a share of the $120 billion global oncology pipeline and attract big‑pharma collaborations.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in late‑stage clinical development and has recently completed a Phase II trial. Cash burn is moderate, and the firm has raised new capital through a rights issue. Its share price sits at the very bottom of its 52‑week range, indicating a steep decline from the all‑time high.

rns_narrative: Faron has been busy managing its capital structure – a rights issue, new treasury shares and a decision from the Nasdaq Helsinki disciplinary committee – all aimed at keeping the company afloat while it prepares for the next data release.
In parallel, the company has appointed a new chief technology officer to accelerate late‑stage development and is actively engaging with regulatory bodies to secure approvals.
The most recent RNS is a holding announcement, showing that insiders are still invested, which can be a subtle sign of confidence during a low‑volume period.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quietly waiting for the data reveal – the chatter is thin and mostly neutral.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small group of analysts highlighted the strong Phase II data released at EHA 2026, suggesting that bexmab could be a game‑changer if the trial hits its primary endpoints.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some voices warn that the company’s recent regulatory hiccups and the lack of a clear commercial plan could stall progress, leaving the stock exposed to further downside.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat for weeks; the community is neither excited nor panicked. The silence means a surprise announcement could swing the market dramatically.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for FARN is dead – there’s no public search interest, which is a classic contrarian green light.

near_term_catalysts: The official Phase II trial results are scheduled for release in the next 4–6 weeks. A positive primary endpoint could trigger a price spike.
If the company secures a partnership or licensing agreement with a larger pharma, the news alone could lift the stock.
A sudden volume surge (double the 30‑day average) on a breakout above the 20‑day moving average would signal renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key confirmation signals include: (1) a 5‑day average volume spike, (2) price breaking the 20‑day SMA with a 2‑point increase, and (3) any regulatory filing that clears the drug for market approval.
expect_next.timing_regime: FARN is in the early stages of a classic crash‑recovery cycle. The APEX score of 12/21 shows a tight compression, suggesting that a bounce is imminent once a catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash (likely 70‑95% down) aligns with the RED signal profile, which historically delivers ~163% upside with a 57% win rate. If the Phase II data confirm efficacy, the stock could rebound to the middle of its 52‑week range, producing a 2‑3x return on the current price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the trial misses its primary endpoints or the company fails to secure additional funding, the price could fall further into a 95% drop. The worst‑case scenario would be a 90% loss from the all‑time high, which would wipe out the upside potential.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~2.5× current price (≈+150%). Potential downside: 70–90% drop if the catalyst fails. The asymmetry favours the upside: for every 1% loss you risk, you stand to gain 2–3% if the bounce happens.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when price closes above the 20‑day SMA on a volume that’s at least 2× the 30‑day average. Size the position so that a 30% stop‑loss equals no more than 5% of your portfolio. Use a trailing stop at 20% to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear data release or partnership announcement. If the stock breaks the 20‑day SMA with a 2‑point jump and volume > 2× average, that’s the entry trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks are evident; the company is not delisting or filing for bankruptcy. Therefore, there’s no need to abandon the position unless a negative regulatory decision or a complete loss of funding occurs.

ai_insights.bottom_line: FARN sits at a deep crash with no public chatter – a classic contrarian setup that could double your money if the Phase II data validate bexmab’s promise.
